docs.title: "Celery + Redis ingestion worker"
docs.excerpt: Document and URL ingestion run as Celery tasks against a Redis broker, not as inline FastAPI background tasks. Each task wraps the async ingestion pipeline with `asyncio.run()` so the same code can be

docs.title: "Observability stack: structured JSON logs, Sentry, request IDs"
docs.excerpt: Stdlib `logging` with a custom JSON formatter (NOT structlog), context vars carrying `request_id / tenant_id / user_id` into every log line, automatic redaction of secret-shaped values, an `x-request-

docs.title: "Principal-based tenant isolation"
docs.excerpt: `Principal` is the immutable view of "who is making this request" derived from a verified JWT or API key. Every tenant-scoped database call MUST source `tenant_id` from a `Principal` — never from path

docs.title: "Fixed-window rate limiting"
docs.excerpt: Rate limiting uses a **fixed-window** counter (not token-bucket), implemented as a Protocol with two backends: in-memory (single-replica dev) and Redis (multi-replica production). Plan-tier limits are

docs.title: "SSRF defense for tenant-supplied URLs"
docs.excerpt: Tenants can hand us a URL we then fetch (URL ingestion of documents; outbound webhook delivery to a customer-supplied callback). Without defense this is textbook SSRF: the attacker can target our inte

docs.title: "Stripe webhook idempotency via Postgres"
docs.excerpt: Stripe delivers webhook events at-least-once. We make our handler idempotent by recording every received `event.id` in a Postgres `public.stripe_events` table whose primary key is the event id. Insert

docs.title: "Subagent-driven execution gotchas"
docs.excerpt: When the orchestrator dispatches a subagent (via the `Agent` tool) to execute a multi-task plan, four failure modes recur often enough to warrant a stable boilerplate. They are independent of which su

docs.title: "Decision: BlobStore handoff between API and Celery worker"
docs.excerpt: API and Celery worker hand uploaded files between processes via a `BlobStore` Protocol that emits `scheme://bucket/key` URIs (`file://…` in dev, `supabase://…` in prod). The previous design — FastAPI

docs.title: "Decision: Vercel + Fly + Upstash + Supabase Storage deploy"
docs.excerpt: Production deploy targets: **Vercel** for the Next.js dashboard, **Fly Machines** for both API and Celery worker (single Dockerfile, two `fly.toml` files, `PROCESS_TYPE` switch), **Upstash Redis** for

docs.title: "Decision: Postgres + MongoDB storage split (mid-migration state)"
docs.excerpt: The data model is split between Supabase Postgres and MongoDB Atlas, but the split is **partial and mid-migration**, not clean. Supabase Postgres is the authoritative store for new identity / billing

docs.title: "Decision: Pydantic AI over LangChain"
docs.excerpt: We chose Pydantic AI for the RAG agent instead of LangChain because it fits FastAPI naturally, has a far smaller surface area, and treats type safety as a first-class concern. The agent layer needed:

docs.title: "Decision: Supabase Auth as primary, NextAuth retained as fallback"
docs.excerpt: The dashboard uses Supabase Auth (`@supabase/ssr`, email/password) and the FastAPI backend verifies the resulting JWT against the Supabase JWKS endpoint. **However, the legacy NextAuth HS256 path is s

docs.title: "Feature: Conversation analytics dashboard"
docs.excerpt: Per-tenant dashboard surfacing chat metrics: conversations, user queries, no-answer rate, top queries, daily timeseries, and a paginated query log with conversation-detail drill-down. Backed by MongoD

docs.title: "Feature: API Key Management"
docs.excerpt: Customers create and revoke API keys for the embeddable widget and programmatic access. Keys carry the `mrag_` prefix, are SHA-256 hashed at rest, and the management endpoints are intentionally JWT-on

docs.title: "Feature: Multi-tenant bot configuration"
docs.excerpt: Each tenant can define multiple chatbots, each with its own system prompt, model selection, document filter, and widget styling. The embeddable widget picks a bot via `data-bot-id`, and the API has a

docs.title: "Feature: Document Ingestion"
docs.excerpt: The pipeline that turns customer-uploaded documents into searchable chunks. Docling parses many formats into markdown, the HybridChunker splits on semantic boundaries, embeddings are generated in batc

docs.title: "Feature: Embeddable chat widget"
docs.excerpt: A self-contained JavaScript chat widget that customers embed on their own site with a single `<script>` tag. The widget streams chat responses from the MongoRAG backend via SSE, isolates its CSS from

docs.title: "Feature: Outbound webhooks"
docs.excerpt: Tenants register HTTPS endpoints to receive event notifications (e.g. `document.created`, `bot.updated`). Events are POSTed with an HMAC-SHA256 signature in a Stripe-style header, retried with exponen

docs.title: "Feature: RAG Agent"
docs.excerpt: The customer-facing chatbot. A Pydantic AI agent with hybrid-search tools that answers questions grounded in tenant documents. Exposed via `/api/v1/chat` with both streaming SSE and synchronous respon

docs.title: "Feature: RAG quality evaluation harness"

docs.title: "Feature: RAG pipeline enhancements (rerank, query rewrite, citations)"
docs.excerpt: Three retrieval-quality features were added on top of hybrid RRF search: a pluggable reranker, optional query rewriting, and inline `[n]`-style citations resolved to a structured Pydantic model. They

docs.title: "Feature: Stripe Billing"
docs.excerpt: Stripe-powered subscription billing for the MongoRAG SaaS. Design spec and implementation plan are merged (PRs #10); implementation is in progress. Tracks subscriptions per tenant, meters usage (queri

docs.title: "Feature: Team management & RBAC"
docs.excerpt: Each tenant has multiple users with one of four roles: `owner / admin / member / viewer`. Owners are protected against accidental removal (last-owner check). Invitations are SHA-256-hashed single-use

docs.title: "Feature: Usage Metering and Rate Limiting"
docs.excerpt: Plan-tier usage metering and rate limiting for the MongoRAG SaaS. Tracks per-tenant, per-period counters (queries, documents, chunks, embedding tokens) in MongoDB and enforces both per-minute request

docs.title: "Hybrid RRF Search"

docs.title: "Multi-Tenancy: Tenant Isolation"
docs.excerpt: MongoRAG runs all customers on a shared MongoDB cluster. Tenant isolation is enforced at the **query layer**, not by separate databases. Every document in tenant-scoped collections carries a `tenant_i

docs.title: testWebApp — Widget Test Host
docs.excerpt: `apps/testWebApp` is a third Next.js application in the monorepo (alongside `apps/web` and `apps/api`), running on **port 3101**. It exists for one purpose: serve a minimal, no-auth, no-CSP-nonce HTML
